title: Udayamperoor Perumthrikkovil Temple
tagline: Udayamperoor Perumthrikkovil Temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Udayamperoor Ekadasi Perumthrikovil Temple is an ancient Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva at Udayamperoor of Ernakulam District in Kerala state in India.
overview.description: Udayamperoor Ekadasi Perumthrikovil Temple is an ancient Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva at Udayamperoor of Ernakulam District in Kerala state in India. This temple is a classic example of the architectural style of Kerala and has monumental towers and Round Sanctum Sanctorum. One of the big Siva temples in Kerala, the Udayamperoor Temple has a courtyard of about four acres of land. The Mukha Mandapa - the first chamber is built in shaped kerala-dravidian architecture.

topPlaces.name: Kureekkad
topPlaces.description: Kureekkad is a census town in Ernakulam district in the Indian state of Kerala. It is the gateway to two of the many suburbs of Kochi, Chottanikkara and Kandanad.

topPlaces.name: St. Mary's Orthodox Syrian Cathedral, Kandanad
topPlaces.description: Kandanad Valiyapally St. Mary's Orthodox Cathedral, Kandanad is a cathedral in the Kandanad village of Ernakulam District of Kerala.

image.alt: St. Mary's Orthodox Syrian Cathedral, Kandanad

topPlaces.name: Thrippunithura
topPlaces.description: Thrippunithura or Tripunithura (Malayalam:തൃപ്പൂണിത്തുറ) is a prominent historical and residential region in the city of Kochi in Kerala, India. Located about 7 km (4 mi) from the city centre, Tripunithura was the capital of the erstwhile Kingdom of Cochin.

topPlaces.name: Thiruvankulam
topPlaces.description: Thiruvankulam is a census town in Thrippunithura municipality, in Ernakulam district, Kerala, India. The area is a part of the Kochi metropolitan area.

topPlaces.name: Sree Poornathrayeesa Temple
topPlaces.description: Sree Poornathrayesa temple (in Malayalam: ശ്രീ പൂര്‍ണ്ണത്രയീശ ക്ഷേത്രം) is a Hindu temple situated in Thrippunithura, Kochi, the capital of the former Kingdom of Cochin, Kerala, India. The temple is considered among the greatest temples in Kerala and was the first among eight royal temples of the erstwhile Kingdom of Cochin.

topPlaces.name: St. George Jacobite Syrian Cathedral, Karingachira
topPlaces.description: Karingachira St. George's Jacobite Syrian Church of Jacobite Syrian Christian Church, built in 722 AD (Makaram 13) by Palathinkal and Maliekal family, is one of the ancient churches of the Syriac Orthodox Church.

image.alt: St. George Jacobite Syrian Cathedral, Karingachira

topPlaces.name: Thrippunithura Terminal metro station
topPlaces.description: Thrippunithura Terminal is a terminal metro station serving as the southern terminal of the Kochi Metro which was opened on 6 March 2024. It was inaugurated by the Prime Minister of India Narendra Modi on 6 March 2024 and marked the completion of final station of the first phase of the Kochi metro system that starts from Aluva.

topPlaces.name: Poonithura
topPlaces.description: Poonithura is a ward of Kochi, Kerala. It features one of the popular temples in Ernakulam, the Poonithura Sree Krishna Swamy Temple, which is the "Moolasthanam" (original place) of Lord Sree Poornathrayeesa, the national deity of the erstwhile Kingdom of Kochi and the guardian of Tripunithura.

The temple is considered among the greatest temples in Kerala and was the first among eight royal temples of the erstwhile Kingdom of Cochin.

items.note: Karingachira St. George's Jacobite Syrian Church of Jacobite Syrian Christian Church, built in 722 AD (Makaram 13) by Palathinkal and Maliekal family, is one of the ancient churches of the Syriac Orthodox Church.

itinerary.title: Day 3 — Thrippunithura Terminal metro station & around

items.note: Thrippunithura Terminal is a terminal metro station serving as the southern terminal of the Kochi Metro which was opened on 6 March 2024. It was inaugurated by the Prime Minister of India Narendra Modi on 6 March 2024 and marked the completion of final station of the first phase of the Kochi metro system that starts from Aluva.

Poonithura is a ward of Kochi, Kerala. It features one of the popular temples in Ernakulam, the Poonithura Sree Krishna Swamy Temple, which is the "Moolasthanam" (original place) of Lord Sree Poornathrayeesa, the national deity of the erstwhile Kingdom of Kochi and the guardian of Tripunithura.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Udayamperoor Perumthrikkovil Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Udayamperoor Perumthrikkovil Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Udayamperoor Perumthrikkovil Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (26 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (10 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Udayamperoor Perumthrikkovil Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Udayamperoor Perumthrikkovil Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Udayamperoor Perumthrikkovil Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Udayamperoor Perumthrikkovil Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Udayamperoor Perumthrikkovil Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kureekkad, St. Mary's Orthodox Syrian Cathedral, Kandanad, Thrippunithura, Thiruvankulam, Sree Poornathrayeesa Temple.
